extracted_items.value: Every day will include ascents and descents. Most days involve long trekking durations.

value: The steep ascent to Bali Pass requires help from an expert guide.
While there are no crevasses, heavy snow makes an ice axe useful.
Use a scissor-bend approach instead of a straight-line climb.
The famous knife-edge ridge leading to Bali Pass is a tricky traversal. With deep valleys on either side of the ridge, the walk can be nerve wracking.
The climb to Advance Base Camp is extremely steep and involves trekking on huge boulders.
The descent is extremely steep beyond the pass.
The entire day from Advance Base Camp to Lower Damini across the Bali Pass is long and arduous.
The trail runs along rivers, requiring frequent crossings.
Bridges may be washed away, needing a rope and pulley system.

evidence.quote_or_summary: Terrain: The steep ascent to Bali Pass requires help from an expert guide. While there are no crevasses, heavy snow makes an ice axe useful. Use a scissor-bend approach instead of a straight-line climb. The famous knife-edge ridge leading to Bali Pass is a tricky traversal. With deep valleys on either side of the ridge, the walk can be nerve wracking. The climb to Advance Base Camp is extremely steep and involves trekking on huge boulders. The descent is extremely steep beyond the pass. The entire day from Advance Base Camp to Lower Damini across the Bali Pass is long and arduous. The trail runs along rivers, requiring frequent crossings. Bridges may be washed away, needing a rope and pulley system.

extracted_items.value: retrace your way back to Taluka and Kotgaon

evidence.quote_or_summary: Before crossing the Bali pass, your best bet is to retrace your way back to Taluka and Kotgaon.

extracted_items.value: descend from Lower Damini and exit at Janakichatti

evidence.quote_or_summary: After crossing the Bali Pass, you can descend from Lower Damini and exit at Janakichatti.
Evacuation may take up to 24 hours since you are deep in the valley and the roads connecting Kotgaon, Purola, Janakichatti and Rajgarhi are notoriously bad.

extracted_items.value: notoriously bad connecting Kotgaon, Purola, Janakichatti and Rajgarhi

evidence.quote_or_summary: the roads connecting Kotgaon, Purola, Janakichatti and Rajgarhi are notoriously bad.
From Taluka, you can hire a jeep to Purola. From Janakichatti, you can hire a jeep to Rajgarhi.

value: Mori
Purola
Primary Healthcare Center at Janakichatti

evidence.quote_or_summary: For mild medical issues, the closest medical assistance can be found in Mori and then in Purola on the Kotgaon side. On the Yamunotri side, there is a Primary Healthcare Center at Janakichatti itself.

value: Mussoorie
Dehradun

evidence.quote_or_summary: for major medical emergencies, the nearest hospital is in Mussoorie or Dehradun
which is almost 6-7 hours away from both Kotgaon and Janakichatti.
Suitable for Experienced Trekkers

value: High altitude pass crossing at 16,207 ft
Knife-edge ridge with deep valleys on either side
Trekking on huge boulders to Advance Base Camp
Frequent river crossings
Snow trek possibility

evidence.quote_or_summary: At 16,207 ft, Bali Pass is one of the highest treks we run at Indiahikes.
The famous knife-edge ridge leading to Bali Pass is a tricky traversal. With deep valleys on either side of the ridge, the walk can be nerve wracking.
The climb to Advance Base Camp is extremely steep and involves trekking on huge boulders.
The trail runs along rivers, requiring frequent crossings.
On the Bali Pass trek, snow is a distinct possibility, especially during the summer.

value.ams_can_affect: anybody - first-time trekkers as well as experienced trekkers

evidence.quote_or_summary: At 16,207 ft, Bali Pass is one of the highest treks we run at Indiahikes. Proper acclimatization is crucial for a successful trek.
At altitudes above 10,000 ft, the chances of being hit by Acute Mountain Sickness are real. Altitude sickness can happen to anybody - first-time trekkers as well as experienced trekkers.

value: Steep ascent to Bali Pass requires expert guide
Knife-edge ridge with deep valleys on either side
Extremely steep climb to Advance Base Camp on huge boulders
Extremely steep descent beyond the pass

evidence.quote_or_summary: Terrain: The steep ascent to Bali Pass requires help from an expert guide. ... The famous knife-edge ridge ... deep valleys on either side ... The climb to Advance Base Camp is extremely steep and involves trekking on huge boulders. The descent is extremely steep beyond the pass. The entire day from Advance Base Camp to Lower Damini across the Bali Pass is long and arduous. The trail runs along rivers, requiring frequent crossings. Bridges may be washed away, needing a rope and pulley system.

value: Snow can make pass inaccessible
Rain makes trails slushy and difficult to navigate safely
Weather deterioration may require turning back from any campsite

evidence.quote_or_summary: Too much snow can make the pass inaccessible.
Rain can turn the trails slushy and difficult to navigate safely.
If the weather deteriorates, the team may need to turn back from any campsite to ensure the safety of all trekkers.

value: Evacuation may take up to 24 hours

Nearest hospital for major emergencies is in Mussoorie or Dehradun, 6-7 hours away
Basic medical centers can only treat very basic injuries like simple fractures, sprains etc.

evidence.quote_or_summary: Evacuation may take up to 24 hours since you are deep in the valley and the roads connecting Kotgaon, Purola, Janakichatti and Rajgarhi are notoriously bad.
for major medical emergencies, the nearest hospital is in Mussoorie or Dehradun – which is almost 6-7 hours away from both Kotgaon and Janakichatti.
these medical centers can only treat very basic injuries like simple fractures, sprains etc.

value: Trek Leaders and Technical Guides provide necessary guidance, support, and technical gear
Technical team and Trek Leader constantly monitor weather conditions
Team equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers
Trekkers must inform Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions

evidence.quote_or_summary: our Trek Leaders and Technical Guides will provide you with all necessary guidance, support, and technical gear such as micro spikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes, etc. wherever required.
Our technical team, in conjunction with the Trek Leader, constantly monitors the weather conditions and makes informed decisions about proceeding with or aborting the trek.
The Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers to handle any altitude-related emergencies.
Please inform your Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions or health concerns, no matter how minor they may seem.
